description: CLI tool to convert Microsoft 365 email threads into linear Markdown dialogues

tasks.name: Initialize Go module
tasks.prompt: Run `go mod init github.com/victor/email-linearize` in the project root.

tasks.name: Create directory structure
tasks.prompt: Create the directory structure: cmd/email-linearize/, internal/{domain,auth,graph,thread,quote,linearize,render}/, testdata/. Add a placeholder .gitkeep in each empty directory.

tasks.name: Add .gitignore
tasks.prompt: Create .gitignore with entries for: Go binary (email-linearize), output/ directory, .build-state.json, token cache files (*.token, .token-cache), vendor/, .DS_Store.

tasks.name: Define domain types
Implement device code flow
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/auth/devicecode.go implementing OAuth 2.0 device code flow:
- DeviceCodeFlow(ctx, clientID, tenantID string) — POST to https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/devicecode with scope=Mail.Read offline_access
- PollForToken(ctx, clientID, tenantID, deviceCode string, interval int) — poll token endpoint until authorized or timeout
- Return TokenResponse{AccessToken, RefreshToken, ExpiresIn}
- Read EMAIL_LINEAR_CLIENT_ID and EMAIL_LINEAR_TENANT_ID from env.
- Never log tokens. Use context.Context for cancellation.

tasks.name: Implement token cache
tasks.prompt:
Create token caching:
- cache.go: TokenCache interface with Save(token TokenResponse) error, Load() (TokenResponse, error), Clear() error
- keychain.go: KeychainCache struct implementing TokenCache using github.com/zalando/go-keyring for OS keychain storage (macOS Keychain, Linux secret-service). Store service='email-linearize', user='default'.
- filecache.go: FileCache struct implementing TokenCache as fallback — writes JSON to ~/.email-linearize/token.enc with file permissions 0600. Use a simple encryption envelope (AES-GCM with a machine-derived key).

tasks.name: Implement token source
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/auth/tokensource.go:
- TokenSource struct wrapping clientID, tenantID, cache TokenCache
- Token(ctx) (string, error): load from cache → if valid, return access token → if expired but refresh token exists, refresh → if refresh fails, run device code flow → save to cache → return access token
- RefreshToken(ctx, refreshToken string) (TokenResponse, error): POST to token endpoint with grant_type=refresh_token
- On refresh failure, return error prompting re-authentication (do not retry infinitely).

tasks.name: Auth module tests
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/auth/auth_test.go:
- TestTokenSource_ReturnsCachedToken: cache has valid token → returns it without HTTP call
- TestTokenSource_RefreshesExpiredToken: cached token expired, refresh token valid → refreshes via httptest.Server
- TestTokenSource_FallsBackToDeviceCode: no cached token → initiates device code flow (mock server)
- TestTokenSource_FailsOnRefreshError: refresh fails → returns error with re-auth message
- Use httptest.Server for all HTTP interactions. Never real credentials.

tasks.name: Implement Graph client
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/graph/client.go:
- Client struct: baseURL string, httpClient *http.Client, tokenSource (func(ctx) (string,error))
- NewClient(baseURL, tokenSource) *Client — baseURL defaults to https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0 but is configurable for tests
- doRequest(ctx, method, path, query) (*http.Response, error) — sets Authorization header, handles JSON
- ResolveFolderID(ctx, folderName) (string, error) — GET /me/mailFolders, match displayName
- FetchMessages(ctx, folderID string, opts FetchOptions) ([]domain.Message, error) — paginate via @odata.nextLink, $select needed fields, $orderby=receivedDateTime asc
- FetchThreadMessages(ctx, conversationID) ([]domain.Message, error) — filter by conversationId
- $top=50 per page

tasks.name: Implement Graph error handling
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/graph/errors.go:
- Handle 401: call token refresh, retry request once. If still 401, return auth error.
- Handle 429: read Retry-After header, sleep, retry.
- Handle 500/503: exponential backoff, max 3 retries.
- Handle 404: return typed NotFoundError.
- Handle 403: return typed PermissionError with message about re-consenting.
- Never log response bodies (contain email content). Log URL, status, duration only when verbose.

tasks.name: Graph client tests
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/graph/graph_test.go:
- TestFetchMessages_PaginatesCorrectly: httptest server returns page1 with nextLink, then page2 without → client returns all messages
- TestFetchMessages_Retries429: server returns 429 with Retry-After:1, then 200 → client waits and succeeds
- TestFetchMessages_Refreshes401: server returns 401, mock token refresh succeeds, retry returns 200
- TestResolveFolderID_FindsInbox: server returns folder list → matches 'Inbox'
- TestResolveFolderID_NotFound: no matching folder → returns error
- Use testdata/ fixtures for response JSON.

tasks.name: Implement thread builder
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/thread/builder.go:
- GroupByConversation(messages []domain.Message) map[string][]domain.Message — group by ConversationID
- BuildThread(conversationID string, messages []domain.Message) domain.Thread — sort by ReceivedDateTime ascending, set Subject from first message
- AssignOrdinals(thread domain.Thread) []domain.AnnotatedMessage — assign Ordinal 1,2,3... to each message, initialize empty QuoteRegions and CleanBody=Body.Content

tasks.name: Thread builder tests
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/thread/thread_test.go:
- TestGroupByConversation_GroupsCorrectly: mixed messages from 2 conversations → 2 groups
- TestBuildThread_SortsByDate: messages in random order → sorted ascending by ReceivedDateTime
- TestBuildThread_SingleMessage: one message → thread with one message
- TestAssignOrdinals_NumbersSequentially: 3 messages → ordinals 1,2,3
- TestBuildThread_IdenticalTimestamps: messages with same timestamp → stable ordering
- Use builder helper to create test messages.

tasks.name: Implement HTML-to-text stripping
tasks.prompt: Create internal/quote/htmlstrip.go: - StripHTML(htmlContent string) string — use golang.org/x/net/html tokenizer to extract text content from HTML, preserving paragraph breaks as newlines - Ignore

tasks.name: Implement quote detection strategies
tasks.prompt:
Create the quote detection system:
- detector.go: Detect(msg domain.AnnotatedMessage, earlier []domain.AnnotatedMessage) []domain.QuoteRegion — runs all strategies, merges overlapping regions, deduplicates
- blockquote.go: detectBlockquotes(htmlContent string) []domain.QuoteRegion — find <blockquote> elements, extract their text ranges
- lineprefix.go: detectLinePrefixQuotes(textContent string) []domain.QuoteRegion — find lines starting with > (and nested >> etc)
- headerpattern.go: detectHeaderQuotes(textContent string) []domain.QuoteRegion — match 'On {date}, {name} wrote:' and 'From: ... Sent: ... Subject: ...' patterns, capture everything after the header until the next non-quoted section
- contenthash.go: attributeQuote(quoteText string, earlier []domain.AnnotatedMessage) *int — normalize and hash quote text, compare against normalized earlier messages, return ordinal of match or nil

tasks.name: Quote detector tests
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/quote/quote_test.go:
- TestDetect_HTMLBlockquote: message with <blockquote> → detected as quote region
- TestDetect_PlainTextPrefix: message with > lines → detected as quote region
- TestDetect_OutlookHeader: 'From: Alice\nSent: ...\nSubject: ...' → detected
- TestDetect_GmailHeader: 'On Jan 14, 2025, Alice wrote:' → detected
- TestDetect_AttributesToCorrectMessage: quote matching message #2 → attributed to ordinal 2
- TestDetect_UnattributableQuote: quote not matching any earlier → AttributedToOrdinal is nil
- TestDetect_NestedQuotes: quote containing another quote → both detected
- TestStripHTML_PreservesText: HTML with paragraphs → clean text with newlines
- TestStripHTML_IgnoresScripts: <script> content stripped
- Use builder helpers for test messages.

tasks.name: Implement linearizer
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/linearize/linearize.go:
- Linearize(thread domain.Thread, annotated []domain.AnnotatedMessage) domain.LinearizedThread
- For each message: run quote detection against earlier messages, then replace each QuoteRegion in the body with a human-readable reference
- Reference format for attributed quotes: [→ see msg #N from {FirstName}, {DD Mon}] where FirstName is sender's first name and DD Mon is day+month of the original message
- Reference format for unattributed quotes: [→ quoted text omitted]
- After replacement: trim consecutive blank lines to at most one, trim trailing whitespace
- Store result in AnnotatedMessage.CleanBody
- Return LinearizedThread with all processed messages

tasks.name: Linearizer tests
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/linearize/linearize_test.go:
- TestLinearize_ReplacesQuoteWithReference: reply quoting msg #1 → body contains '[→ see msg #1 from Alice, 14 Jan]'
- TestLinearize_UnattributedQuote: unmatched quote → '[→ quoted text omitted]'
- TestLinearize_MultipleQuotes: message quoting #1 and #2 → both replaced
- TestLinearize_NoQuotes: message with no quotes → body unchanged
- TestLinearize_TrimsBlankLines: replacement leaves 3 blank lines → collapsed to 1
- TestLinearize_NestedQuotes: quote-within-quote → both replaced with references
- TestLinearize_PreservesAuthorText: text before and after quote preserved

tasks.name: Implement Markdown renderer
Implement text renderer
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/render/text.go:
- RenderText(thread domain.LinearizedThread) string — plain text variant
- Similar structure but without Markdown formatting (no #, **, etc)
- Use '====' separators between messages

tasks.name: Implement file writer
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/render/writer.go:
- WriteFile(outputDir string, thread domain.LinearizedThread, format string) (string, error)
- Slugify subject for filename: lowercase, replace non-alphanumeric with hyphens, collapse consecutive hyphens, max 80 chars, trim trailing hyphens
- Collision safety: if file exists, append -2, -3, etc.
- Create outputDir if it doesn't exist (os.MkdirAll with 0755)
- Write file with permissions 0644
- Return the written file path

tasks.name: Renderer tests
tasks.prompt:
Create internal/render/render_test.go:
- TestRenderMarkdown_StructureCorrect: thread with 2 messages → output matches expected Markdown format
- TestRenderMarkdown_OmitsCcWhenEmpty: message with no Cc → no Cc line
- TestRenderText_NoMarkdownSyntax: output contains no # or ** characters
- TestSlugify_SpecialChars: 'Re: Q4 Budget!! Review' → 're-q4-budget-review'
- TestSlugify_LongSubject: 100-char subject → truncated to 80
- TestWriteFile_CollisionSafety: write same slug twice → second file has '-2' suffix
- Use t.TempDir() for file write tests.

tasks.name: Implement main with flag parsing
tasks.prompt:
Create cmd/email-linearize/main.go:
- Parse flags: --folder (default 'Inbox'), --thread (string), --output (default './output'), --limit (int, default 0=unlimited), --format (default 'markdown'), --verbose (bool), --auth-only (bool)
- Pipeline: create token source → if --auth-only, authenticate and exit → create Graph client → resolve folder ID → fetch messages (apply --limit) → group by conversation → if --thread specified, filter to that conversation → for each thread: build → assign ordinals → linearize → render → write file
- Print summary to stdout: 'Processed N threads, wrote M files to {outputDir}'
- Errors to stderr, exit code 1
- Verbose: log folder resolution, thread count, messages per thread (never email bodies/tokens)

tasks.name: CLI tests
tasks.prompt:
Create cmd/email-linearize/main_test.go:
- TestFlagParsing_Defaults: no flags → folder='Inbox', output='./output', format='markdown'
- TestFlagParsing_CustomValues: all flags set → parsed correctly
- TestFlagParsing_InvalidFormat: --format=xml → error
- Extract flag parsing into a parseFlags function to make it testable without running main().

tasks.name: Create test fixture JSON files
tasks.prompt:
Create test fixture JSON files in testdata/:
- simple_thread.json: Graph API response with 2 messages sharing a conversationId, second message quotes the first
- deep_thread.json: 5-message thread with progressive nesting
- html_thread.json: messages with HTML bodies containing <blockquote> elements
- plaintext_thread.json: messages with plain-text bodies using > prefix quoting
- mixed_thread.json: mix of HTML and plain-text body types
- edge_cases.json: empty body message, missing subject, single-message thread
- graph_folder_list.json: sample /me/mailFolders response with Inbox, Sent Items, Drafts
- graph_paginated.json: first page with @odata.nextLink pointing to page 2
- graph_paginated_page2.json: second page, no nextLink
All fixtures must match the Microsoft Graph v1.0 response shape from context/API.md.

tasks.name: Build and verify
tasks.prompt: Run: go build ./cmd/email-linearize && go test ./... && go vet ./... — fix any errors.

tasks.name: Create README
tasks.prompt: Create README.md with: project description, prerequisites (Go 1.21+, Azure AD app registration), installation (go install), configuration (env vars), usage examples, example output (from context/PRODUCT.md), security notes.

tasks.name: Security audit
tasks.prompt:
Review all code for security issues per context/SECURITY.md:
- grep for token/credential logging
- grep for email body logging
- verify TLS is never skipped
- verify no remote resources fetched from email HTML
- verify token cache has restrictive permissions
Fix any issues found.
